  "Dr. Mohamed NM Lubani teaches Natural Language Processing (WID3002) in Semester 2, 2026.",

  "NLP is a subfield of AI focused on enabling machines to understand, process, and generate human language.",
  "Synonymous terms for NLP include 'natural language processing', 'computational linguistics', and 'human language technologies'.",
  "NLP is important for handling large volumes of text data, as billions of text data are generated daily across various channels.",
  "Unstructured data is estimated to make up between 80%-90% of all data generated.",
  "Text is the most common form of unstructured data.",
  "Structured data is information that has been organized, such as in databases.",
  "NLP is used in chatbots, search engines, social media profiling, machine translation, spam detection, and sentiment analysis.",
  "In finance, NLP helps address the challenge of unstructured financial data by extracting timely and accurate structured data.",
  "Information Extraction (IE) and Named Entity Recognition (NER) are used in finance to identify financial terms.",
  "Sentiment Analysis is used in finance to analyze CEO's tone in earnings calls.",
  "Bloomberg and Reuters use NLP to extract, analyze, and summarize financial data.",
  "Hedge funds use NLP for market sentiment analysis based on news impact on stock prices.",
  "In social media, NLP helps address the challenge of rapidly spreading fake news and misinformation.",
  "NLP is used for sensitive content detection, hate speech, and toxicity filtering on social media platforms.",
  "Fact-Checking NLP Models, trained on real vs. fake news data, are used for fake news detection.",

  "Morphology is the study of word structure and how morphemes form words.",
  "Syntax deals with the rules governing sentence structure and word order.",
  "Semantics focuses on the meaning of words, phrases, and sentences.",
  "Pragmatics studies how meaning depends on context and speaker intent, including sarcasm and politeness.",
  "Discourse analysis focuses on how sentences connect to form meaningful conversations or texts.",
  "NLP faces challenges due to language ambiguity, the constant evolution of language, and limited data for many languages and dialects.",
  "Bias in AI models can lead to unfair or inaccurate results if training data is biased.",
  "Most NLP models focus on English, and many languages lack large datasets for training AI.",
  "Training large NLP models requires massive computing power and consumes large amounts of electricity, leading to high computational costs and carbon footprint.",
  "The course 'Natural Language Processing' blends foundational theories with practical, hands-on experience in applying computational techniques.",
  "Topics covered in the course include Text Pre-processing, Part-of-Speech tagging, Syntactic Parsing, Semantic Analysis, and Language Modelling.",

json_data: Dr. Mohamed NM Lubani teaches Natural Language Processing (WID3002) in Semester 2, 2026.
Natural Language Processing (NLP) is a set of techniques and algorithms used to analyze and understand human language.
NLP is a subfield of AI focused on enabling machines to understand, process, and generate human language.
Synonymous terms for NLP include 'natural language processing', 'computational linguistics', and 'human language technologies'.
NLP is important for handling large volumes of text data, as billions of text data are generated daily across various channels.
Unstructured data is estimated to make up between 80%-90% of all data generated.
Text is the most common form of unstructured data.
Structured data is information that has been organized, such as in databases.
NLP is used in chatbots, search engines, social media profiling, machine translation, spam detection, and sentiment analysis.
In finance, NLP helps address the challenge of unstructured financial data by extracting timely and accurate structured data.
Information Extraction (IE) and Named Entity Recognition (NER) are used in finance to identify financial terms.
Sentiment Analysis is used in finance to analyze CEO's tone in earnings calls.
Bloomberg and Reuters use NLP to extract, analyze, and summarize financial data.
Hedge funds use NLP for market sentiment analysis based on news impact on stock prices.
In social media, NLP helps address the challenge of rapidly spreading fake news and misinformation.
NLP is used for sensitive content detection, hate speech, and toxicity filtering on social media platforms.
Fact-Checking NLP Models, trained on real vs. fake news data, are used for fake news detection.
Sentiment Analysis and Deep Learning are used for toxicity and hate speech detection, including contextual analysis for sarcasm.
Phonetics studies the physical sounds of speech, while phonology examines how these sounds function in a language.
Morphology is the study of word structure and how morphemes form words.
Syntax deals with the rules governing sentence structure and word order.
Semantics focuses on the meaning of words, phrases, and sentences.
Pragmatics studies how meaning depends on context and speaker intent, including sarcasm and politeness.
Discourse analysis focuses on how sentences connect to form meaningful conversations or texts.
NLP faces challenges due to language ambiguity, the constant evolution of language, and limited data for many languages and dialects.
Bias in AI models can lead to unfair or inaccurate results if training data is biased.
Most NLP models focus on English, and many languages lack large datasets for training AI.
Training large NLP models requires massive computing power and consumes large amounts of electricity, leading to high computational costs and carbon footprint.
The course 'Natural Language Processing' blends foundational theories with practical, hands-on experience in applying computational techniques.
Topics covered in the course include Text Pre-processing, Part-of-Speech tagging, Syntactic Parsing, Semantic Analysis, and Language Modelling.
The course assessment includes assignments, a project, online quizzes, and an open book exam.
